q: How do you refresh the static side of a Spark stream-static join when the lookup table changes, without restarting the whole streaming query?
a: The answer depends entirely on what backs the static side. With Delta Lake you get automatic refresh for free: TahoeFileIndex calls DeltaLog.update() on every micro-batch so the join always sees the latest committed snapshot. No code change needed. With non-Delta file formats (Parquet, JSON, CSV) the file index is resolved once and cached in memory — there is no built-in refresh. Your options in that case are: (1) restart the streaming query — the cleanest guarantee but causes a brief gap; (2) call .cache() on the static DataFrame and, from a separate thread or foreachBatch call, invoke DataFrame.unpersist() followed by re-calling spark.read to rebuild it — this works but introduces a race window where two micro-batches could see different versions; (3) store the lookup data in Delta and use Delta as the static source so you get automatic refresh without any of this complexity. The foreachBatch path is sometimes suggested as a workaround — call REFRESH TABLE inside foreachBatch — but this only refreshes the Hive metastore's file list, not the in-memory index used by the streaming plan, and can still produce missing-file errors during concurrent writes. The operationally safe trade-off: use Delta for any lookup table that must stay current; fall back to a query restart with a bounded checkpoint for everything else.
fj: I'd call REFRESH TABLE inside foreachBatch so the static side picks up changes without a restart.
fs: REFRESH TABLE refreshes the metastore file list but not the in-memory FileIndex baked into the streaming plan — new files can still be missed and concurrent writes can produce missing-file errors. Delta's TahoeFileIndex is the only mechanism that reliably refreshes per micro-batch without a restart.

code.label: Delta static side — automatic per-micro-batch refresh
lines: # Delta: TahoeFileIndex.update() called each micro-batch — no extra code needed

code.label: Non-Delta fallback: unpersist + re-read in foreachBatch (race-aware)
lines: # WARNING: race window between unpersist and re-read; prefer Delta instead
# every N batches, rebuild the static side
